title: Michigan Health Officials Race to Find Source of Parasite Outbreak
subtitle: Over 700 people have fallen ill with a severe stomach infection, but the origin of the parasite remains a mystery.

summary.en: Michigan is currently facing a rapid increase in cases of a parasitic infection called cyclosporiasis. Since late June, more than 700 people have reported symptoms, leading to dozens of hospital visits. Health officials are working hard to identify the source of the outbreak, which is linked to a microscopic parasite. While the infection is not usually deadly, it causes severe diarrhea and dehydration. Authorities are asking the public to wash their produce thoroughly to help prevent further spread.
summary.zh: 密西根州目前正面臨一種名為「環孢子蟲病」的寄生蟲感染病例迅速增加。自六月下旬以來，已有超過七百人通報出現症狀，導致數十人住院。衛生官員正努力查明與這種微觀寄生蟲有關的疫情來源。雖然這種感染通常不會致命，但會引起嚴重的腹瀉與脫水。當局呼籲大眾徹底清洗農產品，以協助防止疫情進一步擴散。

paragraphs.en: Health officials in Michigan are working around the clock to find the cause of a fast-growing health crisis. According to the Michigan Department of Health and Human Services (MDHSS), more than 700 people have become sick with a parasite called Cyclospora cayetanensis since June 22. This infection, known as cyclosporiasis, has caused significant concern across the state, especially in the southeastern region where most cases have been recorded.
paragraphs.zh: 密西根州的衛生官員正日以繼夜地追查一場迅速擴大的健康危機。根據密西根州衛生與公共服務部（MDHSS）的資料，自六月二十二日以來，已有超過七百人感染一種名為「環孢子蟲（Cyclospora cayetanensis）」的寄生蟲。這種被稱為「環孢子蟲病」的感染在全州引起了極大擔憂，特別是在大多數病例集中的東南部地區。

paragraphs.en: This situation has developed very quickly. On June 30, the state reported 170 cases, but that number jumped to 572 by July 4. By July 6, the total count had surpassed 700, with 36 people requiring hospital care. The speed of this increase has put local health departments on high alert as they try to stop the spread of the illness.
paragraphs.zh: 此情況發展極為迅速。六月三十日，該州通報了一百七十起病例，但到了七月四日，數字已躍升至五百七十二起。截至七月六日，總數已超過七百人，其中三十六人需要住院治療。病例增加的速度讓地方衛生部門高度警戒，並致力於阻止疫情擴散。

paragraphs.en: The parasite responsible for this outbreak is microscopic, meaning it is too small to be seen without a special tool. According to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), the infection typically causes watery diarrhea and frequent, sometimes explosive, bowel movements. While the illness is usually not life-threatening, it can be very uncomfortable. The main danger for patients is dehydration, which happens when the body loses too much water. Most people recover after about a week, but for some, the symptoms can last for two weeks or longer.
paragraphs.zh: 導致此次疫情的寄生蟲屬於微觀生物，意即若無特殊工具便無法看見。根據美國疾病管制與預防中心（CDC）的說法，這種感染通常會引起水樣腹瀉，以及頻繁且有時會突然發作的排便。雖然這種疾病通常不會危及生命，但會讓人感到非常不適。患者的主要風險在於脫水，即身體流失過多水分。大多數人在約一週後會康復，但對某些人而言，症狀可能會持續兩週甚至更久。

paragraphs.en: Understanding how this parasite spreads is a key part of the current investigation. The parasite is usually found in human waste and can contaminate water or fresh food. In the past, similar outbreaks have been linked to common grocery items like bagged salad mixes, cilantro, basil, raspberries, snow peas, and green onions. Because of this, health experts are reminding the public that washing produce thoroughly before eating is one of the most important ways to stay safe. Good hand hygiene is also a vital step in preventing the infection from spreading further.
paragraphs.zh: 了解這種寄生蟲的傳播方式是目前調查的關鍵。該寄生蟲通常存在於人類排泄物中，並可能污染水源或新鮮食物。過去，類似的疫情曾與常見的雜貨商品有關，例如袋裝沙拉混合菜、香菜、羅勒、覆盆子、荷蘭豆和青蔥。因此，衛生專家提醒大眾，在食用前徹底清洗農產品是保持安全最重要的途徑之一。良好的手部衛生也是防止感染進一步擴散的關鍵步驟。

paragraphs.en: Despite the large number of cases, the exact source of this specific outbreak remains unknown. A spokesperson for the MDHSS told Ars Technica that no specific grower, supplier, or type of food has been identified as the cause yet. The state is currently leading a massive investigation, working closely with local health departments in the southeastern part of Michigan. Officials are interviewing people who have become sick to see if they all ate the same food or visited the same locations. By sharing this data with the CDC, the state hopes to find a common link that will explain how the parasite is reaching so many people.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管病例數量龐大，但此次疫情的確切來源仍然不明。密西根州衛生與公共服務部的一位發言人向科技媒體「Ars Technica」表示，目前尚未確認任何特定的種植者、供應商或食物種類為致病原因。該州目前正主導一項大規模調查，並與密西根州東南部的地方衛生部門密切合作。官員們正在訪談染病民眾，以確認他們是否都吃了相同的食物或造訪過相同的地點。透過與疾病管制與預防中心分享這些數據，該州希望能找到共同的關聯，以解釋為何這種寄生蟲會影響這麼多人。

paragraphs.en: This investigation is a complex process. Because the parasite can be carried on many different types of fresh produce, finding the "patient zero" or the specific contaminated item is like looking for a needle in a haystack. Investigators must compare the shopping and eating habits of hundreds of people to find a pattern. This requires a high level of coordination between local, state, and national health agencies.
paragraphs.zh: 這項調查是一個複雜的過程。由於寄生蟲可能附著在多種不同的新鮮農產品上，要找到「零號病人」或特定的受污染物品，簡直如同大海撈針。調查人員必須比對數百人的購物與飲食習慣以找出模式。這需要地方、州與國家衛生機構之間高度的協調。

paragraphs.en: As the investigation continues, the focus remains on keeping the public informed and preventing new cases. Health officials have not yet issued a specific warning against any single product, as they do not have enough evidence to point to one source. However, the general advice remains the same: be careful with fresh food and wash your hands often. The MDHSS continues to monitor the situation closely, and they are expected to release more information as soon as they can confirm a source. For those who feel sick, the best advice is to stay hydrated and contact a doctor if symptoms become severe or last for more than a few days. The community is waiting for answers as the state works to bring this outbreak under control.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著調查持續進行，重點仍放在讓大眾獲知最新資訊並預防新病例。衛生官員尚未針對任何單一產品發布特定警告，因為他們沒有足夠的證據指向單一來源。然而，一般的建議仍然相同：處理新鮮食物時要小心，並勤洗手。密西根州衛生與公共服務部持續密切監控情況，預計在確認來源後會盡快發布更多資訊。對於感到不適的民眾，最好的建議是保持水分補充，若症狀嚴重或持續超過幾天，請聯繫醫生。在該州努力控制疫情的同時，社區正等待著答案。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Happening or working continuously, 24 hours a day.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個常見的慣用語，字面上看起來與時鐘有關，但實際是指不間斷地工作。文中：Health officials in Michigan are working around the clock to find the cause of a fast-growing health crisis.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To increase suddenly and by a large amount.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（跳躍），這裡用於描述數據的快速增長。文中：On June 30, the state reported 170 cases, but that number jumped to 572 by July 4.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Connected to or associated with something as a cause.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡表示兩者之間存在因果或關聯關係。文中：In the past, similar outbreaks have been linked to common grocery items like bagged salad mixes, cilantro, basil, raspberries, snow peas, and green onions.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Something that is extremely difficult to find because it is hidden among many other things.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 大海撈針（極難找到的東西）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個比喻用法，形容在大量資訊中尋找特定線索的困難度。文中：Because the parasite can be carried on many different types of fresh produce, finding the "patient zero" or the specific contaminated item is like looking for a needle in a haystack.

sources.name: Ars Technica
sources.title: Michigan sees explosive outbreak of diarrheal parasite with over 700 cases

previousArticles.title: Health Officials Investigate Large Parasite Outbreak in Michigan
previousArticles.summaryEn: Michigan is currently dealing with a fast-growing outbreak of a parasite called Cyclospora. Over 700 cases have been reported to health authorities in just two weeks. While the illness is usually not deadly, it causes severe stomach problems and dehydration. Officials are working hard to find the source of the infection, which often comes from contaminated food or water. For now, experts advise people to wash all fresh produce carefully to stay safe.

quiz.question: What is the primary risk associated with the cyclosporiasis infection mentioned in the article?

options.A: Long-term damage to the digestive system
options.C: The inability to see the parasite without a special tool
options.D: A high mortality rate among those infected

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，雖然該疾病通常不會危及生命，但患者面臨的主要危險是脫水（dehydration），這是因為身體流失了過多水分。選項 A 並未在文中提及會造成長期損傷；選項 C 描述的是寄生蟲的特性，而非感染帶來的健康風險；選項 D 錯誤，因為文中明確提到該疾病通常不會致命（not life-threatening）。

quiz.question: Why is the investigation into the Michigan outbreak considered a 'complex process'?

options.A: Because the parasite is resistant to standard hand-washing techniques
options.B: Because the number of cases is decreasing too quickly to track
options.C: Because the parasite can be transmitted through a wide variety of fresh produce
options.D: Because the CDC has refused to cooperate with local Michigan health officials

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第六段，調查之所以複雜，是因為寄生蟲可能存在於多種不同的新鮮農產品中，這使得找出受污染的特定食物來源如同大海撈針。選項 A 與文意不符，文中強調洗手是預防的重要步驟；選項 B 錯誤，文中提到病例數是在快速增加而非減少；選項 D 與事實相反，文中提到州政府正與 CDC 密切合作。

quiz.question: In the sixth paragraph, what does the phrase 'looking for a needle in a haystack' imply about the investigation?

options.A: It is an extremely difficult task to find a specific item among many possibilities
options.B: The investigators are using specialized tools to scan large fields of produce
options.C: The source of the illness is likely hidden in a rural farming area
options.D: The investigation is moving forward very rapidly and efficiently

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。這個慣用語意指在眾多可能性中尋找一個特定的目標非常困難。在文中，它用來形容在多種農產品中找出確切的污染源是一項艱鉅的挑戰。選項 B 是對該片語的字面誤解；選項 C 錯誤地將其解釋為地理位置；選項 D 則與該片語所表達的「困難與緩慢」之意完全相反。

quiz.question: What is the main message of the article regarding the current health situation in Michigan?

options.A: Michigan officials have successfully identified the contaminated food source
options.B: Cyclosporiasis is a new, deadly disease that requires immediate vaccination
options.C: Health authorities are investigating a widespread outbreak and advising public caution
options.D: The outbreak has been brought under control due to strict new government regulations

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章的核心在於報導密西根州爆發的寄生蟲感染事件，說明當局正在進行大規模調查，同時提醒大眾保持良好的衛生習慣並注意食品安全。選項 A 錯誤，文中多次提到源頭尚未查明；選項 B 錯誤，文中提到該病通常不致命；選項 D 錯誤，目前調查仍在進行中，尚未宣布疫情已完全受控。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
